description:
**Milestone:** Foundation

Create new workspace package @protolabsai/crdt. Wraps Automerge 3 + automerge-repo with a CRDTStore class that manages Automerge documents by domain (features, projects, config). Provides document creation, change, subscribe, and sync lifecycle. Handles persistence to .automaker/crdt/ directory. Includes WebSocket sync adapter configured for Tailscale peer connections.

**Files to Modify:**
- libs/crdt/package.json
- libs/crdt/tsconfig.json
- libs/crdt/src/index.ts
- libs/crdt/src/crdt-store.ts
- libs/crdt/src/sync-adapter.ts
- libs/crdt/src/documents.ts
- libs/crdt/src/types.ts
- package.json
- tsconfig.json
- Dockerfile

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] CRDTStore class creates and manages Automerge documents by domain name
- [ ] change() method applies mutations and emits local change events
- [ ] subscribe() method fires callbacks on local and remote changes
- [ ] WebSocket sync adapter connects to peer via Tailscale IP:port
- [ ] Persistence adapter saves/loads Automerge binary to .automaker/crdt/
- [ ] Two-node integration test: change on node A appears on node B within 200ms
- [ ] Conflict test: concurrent field updates on same document merge correctly
- [ ] Every CRDT document root includes schemaVersion field (integer, starting at 1)
- [ ] Schema-on-read normalizers run on document load (same pattern as FeatureLoader legacy status normalization)
- [ ] On first sync startup, if CRDT storage is empty but filesystem has features/projects, run one-time hydration from filesystem to CRDT documents
- [ ] Basic compaction: periodic Automerge.save() creates compressed checkpoint to prevent unbounded history growth
- [ ] All CRDT operations include instanceId and timestamp for attribution and auditability
- [ ] Package builds and passes typecheck

**Complexity:** large

**Guardrails:** If this phase involves new or changed behavior, include tests that verify correctness. If this phase adds, removes, or changes a user-facing feature, API endpoint, configuration option, or service, update the relevant documentation in `docs/` to reflect those changes.
